channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

  4. Lucy's First Asteroid Flyby Sheds Light on a Double-lobed Asteroid  Sky & Telescope

item.title: NASA picks Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for Mars mission, setting up a race with SpaceX - TechCrunch
item.description:
1. NASA picks Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for Mars mission, setting up a race with SpaceX  TechCrunch
  2. NASA Announces Public-Private Partnership to Advance Mars Science  NASA (.gov)
  3. Relativity Space to privately develop Mars orbiter mission  SpaceNews
  4. In June 2026, Relativity Space — the rocket company Eric Schmidt took over in 2025, whose one rocket has never reached orbit — said it would privately build and fly a Mars orbiter in 2028, carrying NASA's Aeolus atmospheric instruments and doubling as a c  Space Daily
  5. Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt’s Relativity Space selected for upcoming NASA Mars orbiter mission  Scientific American

item.title: 'A mixture from zero to infinity': Physicists tried splitting a photon — and ended up with an improbable swarm of particles - Live Science

  4. 🔪 Cut a photon in two? It creates an infinite number of new photons  Techno-Science.net
  5. Here’s what would happen if you tried to break a photon in half  Science News

item.title: The oldest solid material ever found on Earth didn't come from Earth at all — it was locked inside a meteorite that landed near Murchison, Victoria in 1969, and the stardust grains inside formed roughly 7 billion years ago, before the Sun existed. - Space Daily
item.description: The oldest solid material ever found on Earth didn't come from Earth at all — it was locked inside a meteorite that landed near Murchison, Victoria in 1969, and the stardust grains inside formed roughly 7 billion years ago, before the Sun existed.  Space Daily

item.title: 344 steps stood between the James Webb Space Telescope and total failure — any one could have ended it — and the telescope that survived them all now runs on less power than a household kettle, a million miles from Earth - Space Daily

item.title: Some climate models suggest Venus could once have had liquid water and habitable temperatures, until a dramatic transformation hundreds of millions of years ago. One leading idea is that widespread volcanic resurfacing helped push the planet into the run - Space Daily

item.title: When a honeybee colony outgrows its hive it makes a genuinely democratic decision: scout bees fly out, inspect possible new homes, and 'campaign' for their favourite with waggle dances, lobbying harder for better sites — and only once enough scouts have - Space Daily

item.title: Hundreds of Mysterious Quakes Have Been Detected Deep Under Antarctica - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Hundreds of Mysterious Quakes Have Been Detected Deep Under Antarctica  ScienceAlert

  3. Deep learning helps discover hundreds of Antarctic earthquakes coming from an unlikely location  Phys.org
  4. Researchers discover 500 hidden earthquakes beneath Antarctica: 'Improve our understanding of possible futures'  The Cool Down
  5. Forget the image of a frozen and still continent: scientists have just revealed hundreds of earthquakes beneath East Antarctica, in an area where no one expected to find this phenomenon.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás

item.title: Charles Darwin argued that the roots of human morality were not separate from animal life, and the surprise is what that does to civilization — manners, conscience, and restraint become not the opposite of instinct, but instinct complicated by memory, languag - Space Daily

item.title: Whoever finds the Voyager Golden Record will know exactly how long it has been drifting, because the scientists who built it pressed a small sample of uranium into the cover — a built-in clock that keeps time for roughly a billion years. - Space Daily
item.description: Whoever finds the Voyager Golden Record will know exactly how long it has been drifting, because the scientists who built it pressed a small sample of uranium into the cover — a built-in clock that keeps time for roughly a billion years.  Space Daily

item.title: In December 1938 a museum curator sifting through a fisherman's catch in a South African port lifted out a heavy blue fish that science had known only from fossils and presumed extinct since the age of the dinosaurs - Space Daily
